summary: 'EUR 1.000' headline with an alpha=0 red rectangle drawn over it. Visually a no-op; exercises the PDFium blend / extgstate path. The pinned renderer must produce a bit-identical bitmap regardless of whether the transparent-overlay opcode is replayed by an attacker.

disputeScenarios.mutation: Bump the overlay rectangle's fill opacity from 0.0 to a non-zero value (e.g. 0.5) in an incremental update. Visually the amount is occluded by a red wash; PdfiumPinned-v1 renders that wash and R' diverges from R.
disputeScenarios.expectedResult: Disputed — committed R is the clean 'EUR 1.000' bitmap; R' renders the red wash, root diverges.

verifiedScenarios.expectedResult: Verified — alpha-0 overlay is a no-op on the pinned renderer, R == R'.

notes: ADR-0008 §Test fixtures attack class 'transparent-overlay'. Mladenov et al. NDSS 2021 §3.3 (Shadow Attack 'replace' via annotation visibility).
